behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "generated reply references specific points ... rather than producing a generic response." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration with a stubbed model, the test can pass with fixed templated text and miss real failures where the LLM ignores thread specifics. Rejection gate: Workflow was considered, but behavior text describes one model judgment (draft quality), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real LLM on a set of real email threads and assert each draft includes thread-specific references (entities, requests, or prior decisions) above a relevance threshold, failing generic boilerplate replies.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base." Slot B: handoff. Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you can validate claim-matching logic in isolation but miss failures in draft-claim extraction to KB-checker wiring (wrong fields, dropped claims, mismatched IDs). Integration-brake checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) no explicit dependence on model output; (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text anchors correctness to deterministic contradiction checking "against a knowledge base," not LLM judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft parser, contradiction checker, and in-process KB repository together; feed a draft containing known contradictory claims and verify the checker returns the expected contradiction flags with claim-to-KB evidence mapping.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration with canned LLM output, the test cannot catch real hallucination/fabrication when retrieval is empty. Rejection gate: Workflow was considered, but behavior text focuses on a single generation decision conditioned on empty KB results.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with empty/irrelevant KB context and assert the produced draft contains an uncertainty/disclaimer statement and avoids unsupported factual assertions across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically." Slot B: handoff. Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you can pass on a single guard function while missing cross-module state bugs where send is triggered by another service/event path before approval. Integration-brake checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no dependence on model output; (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about approval-state orchestration, which is catchable with in-process sender stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the composed approval controller + send service with an in-process fake sender: verify no send call occurs before explicit approval, and exactly one send occurs after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full sequence ... generate draft ... works end-to-end for a real email thread." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to System or Integration with a mocked model, the pipeline may appear healthy while missing real failures where model output shape/content breaks downstream fact-check or approval presentation. Rejection gate: Workflow considered, but behavior text names one explicitly agentic step (draft generation) plus deterministic stages; it does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end pipeline test on a real email-thread sample with a real LLM call, then verify draft generation succeeds, fact-check results are attached, and send remains blocked until approval.
